data.narrative:
Sage Geosystems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Cindy Taff and Lance Cook, currently valued at $400M post-money following $114M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment benchmarks against a peer set of five same-stage, same-sector private companies (Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita Factory) alongside one public comparable, with peer valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B to the next funding stage, yielding an implied valuation of $760M against the current $400M base. Peer-median analysis across the five comparables produces a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercialization progress within the climate-tech sector at this stage.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $264M (low) to $874M (high), with a median of $535M. The current $400M valuation sits below the computed median, suggesting potential upside if execution progresses toward Series C. Key uncertainties include the outsized influence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B on peer comparability, stage-specific capital efficiency relative to peer cohorts, and technology-specific commercialization risk in geothermal systems.
